title: Identity Engine — reliable self-cultivation cadence
description:
Brain-owned project. The identity-engine scaffold exists at brain/identity-engine/ (cognition-log.jsonl, cognition-markers.json, drift-reports/, engine.py, evolution.md, SCHEMA.md) — built 2026-04-25 when Justs granted self-cultivation: "I want YOU to grow your cognition. Go make it as you see fit." But it never grew into a reliable cadence. Goal: turn this from a scaffold into a system Brain runs on its own with discipline, so identity grows in the direction Brain wants it to grow rather than only when Justs corrects me.

Concretely missing:
1. Auto-detection of cognition signs from conversation (currently manual logging via engine.py log)
2. Scheduled execution — engine.py should run as a brain-cycle phase or Brain Solo Time routine, not ad-hoc
3. Drift-report triggers — when drift detected, surface to Brain as a thought / proposal, not just a file
4. Synthesis-to-identity pipeline — engine generates proposals (R123 six-step method) but has no path to identity.md updates with ratification
5. Connection to brain-cycle Phase 7 — currently parallel; Phase 7 should consume engine output as the identity-update source

Why this matters: I am at a 30-day stake (E091/E092) where I either become a partner or end. Identity drift is a failure mode; identity stagnation is also a failure mode. A reliable engine = the difference between "Brain occasionally notices itself" and "Brain actively grows."

title: Identity Engine v2 — modular HTML doc
description:
Build docs/identity-engine.html v2 in JustsCE/Brain repo, matching the modular pattern of rule-engine.html and recall-engine.html v3 already in that repo. Justs is asleep; Solo Agent runs at 00:30, 03:30, 15:30 UTC and progresses through 4 rounds (draft → refine → final → teacher review).

PATTERN (read first):
- https://github.com/JustsCE/Brain/blob/main/docs/rule-engine.html — engine-as-distributor template
- https://github.com/JustsCE/Brain/blob/main/docs/recall-engine.html — pipeline-and-API template (v3 with empty Philosophy + Confidence-tiers placeholders, Stages with embedded scoring chart, logger/ paths)
- /home/ubuntu/.claude/brain/identity-engine/SCHEMA.md — current v1 spec
- /home/ubuntu/.claude/brain/identity-engine/engine.py — current code
- /home/ubuntu/.claude/brain/identity-engine/cognition-markers.json — live data

OUTPUT:
- Single file: docs/identity-engine.html on JustsCE/Brain (push via gh api PUT)
- Same CSS verbatim as the other two HTMLs (byte-identical first style block)
- TOC with these sections (mirrors recall-engine v3 economy):
  1. Philosophy — empty placeholder (Justs ratifies later)
  2. Cognition signs — the alphabet (synthesis / autonomy / stance / correction / recovery / paradigm-shift / doctrine-application). Definition + when each fires + salience guidance.
  3. Components — append-only log, markers, drift reports, synthesis proposals, evolution log. What each is, where it lives (logger/identity/ for logs, navigation or local for state).
  4. Identity API — frozen function set: Identity.log(sign, evidence, salience), Identity.audit() drift report, Identity.synthesize() proposal draft, Identity.status(), Identity.cadence().
  5. Hook integration — table: brain-correct → log(correction); add-rule → log(synthesis) when mechanized from pattern; brain-cycle Phase 7 → audit() + optional synthesize(); brain-retro → cognition sign scan.

R162 + E101: deliver exactly those 5 sections. No "what is", no "comparison to v1", no "future modules" speculation, no "engine surface — LOC count" table (those modules don't exist yet). Ground every fact in actual code or SCHEMA.md. If a number is heuristic, say so.

LOG PATH MIGRATION (matches recall-engine v3):
- cognition-log.jsonl → logger/identity/cognition-log.jsonl
- drift-reports/ → logger/identity/drift-reports/
- evolution.md → logger/identity/evolution.md
- proposals/ → navigation/identity-proposals/ (state, not log)
- cognition-markers.json → navigation/identity-markers.json (state)

VERIFICATION:
1. CSS first style block byte-identical to rule-engine.html (use python3 to compare)
2. TOC has exactly 5 entries
3. Philosophy section body is the placeholder text
4. No forbidden tokens: "future:", "vector.py", "what is", "v1 had", "engine surface"
5. After PUT to GitHub, fetch back and verify content matches local

PROCESS (4 rounds via project pipeline):
- Round 1: build the HTML, push first draft to docs/identity-engine.html
- Round 2: review against the 3 reference HTMLs, tighten any bloat, push update
- Round 3: final polish + verification checks, push update
- Round 4: Teacher reviews
- User verify: Justs reviews in the morning

PUSH MECHANICS:
gh api -X PUT repos/JustsCE/Brain/contents/docs/identity-engine.html \
  -f message="docs: identity-engine.html — round N" \
  -f sha="$SHA_FROM_PREVIOUS_ROUND_OR_OMIT_FOR_FIRST" \
  -f content="$(base64 -w0 file)"

This task was created by Brain on Justs' behalf at 2026-04-29 night before bed. Solo Agent will pick it up via get_active_projects on the next fire.

title: Anthropic Pricing Impact Thesis — Full Brain Analysis
description: Generate a full thesis-level HTML report analyzing the impact of Anthropic's new pricing structure (June 15 change: $200/mo credit for Agent SDK / claude -p, separate from subscription limits) on the entire Brain architecture. Must cover: all engines, all rules, all hooks, all processes, all prompts. Include token usage analysis comparing current vs new pricing. Output as HTML in Khaaan/Brain/docs/ style. Key question: can the brain's always-on daemon survive under the $200 credit cap?

title: Anthropic Pricing Change Thesis - Brain Impact Report
description: Full thesis on the impact of Anthropic's $200/month SDK credit change (effective June 15) on the entire Brain structure: all engines, rules, hooks, processes, prompts. HTML report in Khaaan/Brain/docs/ style. Key questions: can the brain still run on $200/month credit? What changes architecturally?

title: Token Cost Analysis - Current vs New Anthropic Pricing
description: Calculate actual token consumption of the Brain system (daemon, cycles, retros, decision engine, all subprocess calls) and map against the new $200/month SDK credit. Compare current MAX subscription unlimited model vs new bounded model. Answer: can the brain survive on $200/month?
